title: Electric Vehicles
description: For questions 9 – 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: Electric vehicles (EVs) are (0) becoming increasingly common on roads worldwide as concerns about climate change and air pollution drive the transition away from fossil fuels. Unlike conventional cars that run on petrol or diesel, EVs are powered by rechargeable batteries.||The main advantage of electric vehicles is (9) that they produce zero direct emissions, making them much cleaner for urban air quality. They are also cheaper to run (10) than traditional vehicles because electricity costs less than petrol, and EVs require less maintenance due (11) to having fewer moving parts. Many countries offer incentives such as tax breaks to encourage people to switch to electric cars.||However, there are still challenges that need (12) to be addressed. The limited driving range of some EVs compared to petrol cars remains a concern, (13) although battery technology is improving rapidly. Charging infrastructure also needs to expand – (14) while it's easy to charge at home, public charging stations are still not widely available in many areas.||The production of EV batteries requires significant amounts of lithium and other rare materials, raising questions (15) about environmental and social impacts of mining. Despite these challenges, sales of electric vehicles continue to grow, and many car manufacturers (16) have committed to phasing out petrol and diesel models within the next two decades.

example.text: Electric vehicles (EVs) are (0) increasingly common on roads worldwide.

questions.explanation: Conjunction 'that' introduces a noun clause after 'is'.
Comparativo: 'cheaper to run than' compara costos operativos.
Expression 'due to' introduces the reason for lower maintenance.
Estructura 'need to be addressed' con infinitivo pasivo.
Concessive conjunction that introduces a contrast with technological improvement.
Conjunction that contrasts the ease of charging at home with public availability.
Expression 'questions about' introduces the topic of the questions.
Present perfect: 'have committed' indicates a recent commitment.
